name: Draft prehab warm-up protocol
notes: Write the standardized team warm-up: dynamic mobility, activation, and movement prep to open every practice.

name: Set up weekly hamstring and ankle prehab circuit
notes: Build the twice-weekly circuit: Nordic curls, banded ankle work, hip hinges, and single-leg balance stations.

name: Schedule baseline movement screens
notes: Coordinate with the head coach to set screening windows by position group during pre-season camp.

name: Log screening results by position group
notes: Record group-level movement screen results and flag emphasis areas for line, skill, and special teams.

name: Order resistance bands and mini-bands
notes: Place the equipment order for resistance bands, mini-bands, and anchor straps for the prehab stations.

name: Receive and inventory foam rollers and mats
notes: Check in the foam roller and mat shipment, label by station, and confirm counts against the order.

name: Stock and label all prehab stations
notes: Set up each station with the right equipment, station cards, and a rotation map ready for practice.

name: Request prehab field time from HC
notes: Submit the weekly field-time request so prehab blocks fit before football practice in the pre-season schedule.

name: Confirm station rotation and time blocks
notes: Finalize the rotation order and time per station with the coaching staff for the first prehab week.

name: Plan Sunday dinner menu at Brenda's
notes: Smothered chicken, greens, mac and cheese, and cornbread; assign dishes after 9:30 service.

name: Grocery run for Sunday dinner
notes: Publix list: chicken, collards, sharp cheddar, buttermilk, and cornmeal.

name: Train assistant coaches on prehab routine
notes: Walk the assistant coaches through cueing, progressions, and how to run each station safely.

name: Certify coaches to lead circuits solo
notes: Sign off each coach to run a circuit unsupervised once they can cue and correct the core drills.

name: Run weekly in-season maintenance circuit
notes: Keep the reduced-volume hamstring and ankle prehab circuit going on practice days through the season.

name: Mark line group compliance on track
notes: Offensive and defensive line hit their weekly prehab completion target; logged as on track for the week.

name: Write team warm-up cue sheet
notes: Document the dynamic warm-up cues and coaching points for the prehab protocol playbook.

name: Document hamstring and ankle circuit stations
notes: Write up each prehab station with sets, reps, and progression notes for the staff playbook.

name: Sunday meal prep for the week
notes: Grill chicken, roast sweet potatoes, and portion overnight oats for early-run breakfasts.

name: Finalize program launch-day checklist
notes: Lock station layout, position-group assignments, and the first-week prehab schedule for launch day.

name: Build athlete buy-in handout
notes: Draft a one-page player and parent handout on why prehab matters and what the weekly routine looks like.
